tests.title: Dance Festival Experience
tests.subtitle: An organiser describes how this dance project developed.
tests.text: Dance Festival Experience became popular in the local community several years ago. The organiser first became interested in dance while helping at a school event. [11]At the beginning, the project was only intended for a small group of friends[/11]. However, more people joined after hearing about it online. Participants now travel from nearby towns to take part each month. The organiser says the most important lesson was learning how to work with different kinds of people. At first, some activities did not go well because planning took longer than expected. Even so, everyone enjoyed learning together and sharing ideas. One volunteer suggested creating shorter activities for beginners, which quickly improved attendance. [12]The organisers realised that newcomers needed simple explanations and patient support[/12]. Over time, the project became more successful and received attention from local newspapers. Teenagers especially enjoyed having opportunities to try something different outside school. Some participants later continued studying dance at college. The organiser was surprised by this because the original goal was simply to create an enjoyable weekend activity. [13]The organiser never expected the project to influence people’s future careers[/13]. Today, meetings usually begin with a short introduction followed by practical activities in small groups. Experienced members often help beginners and explain difficult tasks carefully. Parents sometimes attend open days to see what students have created. Recently, organisers have started sharing videos online because younger members requested more digital content. [14]The organiser believes regular practice is more useful than trying to become perfect immediately[/14]. Although the project has grown, the atmosphere remains relaxed and friendly. Many participants say they enjoy meeting people with similar interests as much as the activities themselves. The organiser hopes the programme will continue expanding in the future. [15]The main aim is to encourage young people to feel confident trying new experiences[/15].

questions.question: What was the project like when it first started?

options.A: It was created for a small number of people.
options.B: It already had members from many towns.
options.C: It was organised by a local newspaper.

questions.explanation: The project originally involved only a few friends.

questions.question: What did the organisers learn?

options.A: Beginners needed clearer support and explanations.
options.B: Activities should become more difficult quickly.

questions.explanation: Organisers understood beginners required patient guidance.

questions.question: Why was the organiser surprised?

options.A: Some participants later chose related careers.
options.B: The project closed for several months.

questions.explanation: The organiser did not expect career influence.

questions.question: What advice does the organiser give?

options.A: Practise regularly instead of expecting perfection immediately.

questions.explanation: The organiser values regular practice most.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the programme?

options.A: To help young people feel confident trying new things.
options.B: To prepare students for difficult exams.

questions.explanation: The programme encourages confidence and new experiences.
